errors: The implementation specifies a 1‑month lookback period for colonoscopy, whereas clinical guidelines for colon‑cancer screening recommend a 10‑year interval between colonoscopies. This mismatch makes the time‑interval value clinically unreasonable.

The assessment lookback period of **1 month** is not clinically reasonable for prostate cancer screening; standard practice recommends a screening interval of **1 year** (or longer, depending on risk factors).

The Minimum Age threshold of 25 years is higher than the typical guideline for hypertension screening, which usually starts at 18 or 21 years of age. Additionally, a 6‑month lookback period for screening is shorter than the commonly recommended 12‑month interval. The Value Set ID (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1920) is not verified against the standard hypertension screening value set and may not be appropriate.

The time interval specified as “9 g%” is not a valid duration; it appears to be a value rather than a lookback period (e.g., days, weeks, or months). The age threshold (≥18 years) and the HbA1c value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.198.11.1024) are clinically reasonable.

The Minimum Age value is incorrectly set to “Age ≤ 75 years”; it should represent the lower bound (≥ 60 years). The upper bound (≤ 75 years) is misplaced and should be defined as a separate Maximum Age criterion.
Age threshold mismatch (CPG recommends screening adults 18+ rather than ≥30); Time interval mismatch (CPG recommends screening every 3 years for low‑risk adults, not annually).
The value “35 kg/m²” is listed under “Time Intervals (Lookback Periods)” but it is a BMI threshold, not a time interval. It should be categorized as a BMI cutoff rather than a lookback period.
Age threshold mismatch – the implementation requires patients to be ≥25 years old for screening, whereas clinical guidelines typically recommend alcohol misuse screening for all adults ≥18 years.
